# 'gs anchor create --anchor BASE' creates a dependent worktree:
# an internal anchor pinned at an existing tracked branch, so the new
# worktree's stack builds on top of another worktree's work.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-03-29T14:00:00Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# Primary builds a stack: main -> featA1
git add featA1.txt
gs bc featA1 -m 'Add featA1'

# A root anchor cannot name the canonical trunk via --anchor.
! gs anchor create $WORK/bad --anchor main
stderr 'canonical trunk'

# --anchor must name a tracked branch.
! gs anchor create $WORK/bad --anchor nope
stderr 'not a tracked branch'

# Create a dependent worktree anchored at featA1.
gs anchor create $WORK/wtB --anchor featA1 -b featB1
stderr 'Created anchor wtB anchored at featA1'
stderr 'Created and checked out branch featB1'

# featA1 remains a normal tracked branch in the primary (not severed).
cd $WORK/repo
gs ls
stderr 'featA1'

# In wtB, featB1 is checked out and stacks on the anchor pointer wtB.
cd $WORK/wtB
git branch --show-current
stdout 'featB1'
gs ls
stderr 'featB1'
stderr 'wtB'

-- repo/featA1.txt --
featA1
